title: 🎙️ Working Lands & Industry
description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Working Lands & Industry' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: precision forestry and remote sensing in Cariboo logging, AgTech and ranch management systems, mining automation and safety technology, farm sensors and soil monitoring, sawmill modernization and automation, drone surveys for resource management, IoT in agriculture and ranching, climate adaptation tech for working lands, workforce training for industrial tech, commodity tracking and supply chain tech, and how Cariboo resource industries adopt innovation. Prefer articles that bridge resource economy AND technology in rural/industrial contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [New Atlas] Dutch timber homes hit carbon-negative milestone with Delft Red reboot
items.content_html:
With the constant pressure for architecture to reduce its massive carbon footprint, studios are always looking for solutions beyond traditional materials. ORGA from the Netherlands has taken on this challenge, rewriting the rulebook by building a carbon-negative neighborhood prototype in the village of Marknesse.

Continue Reading

Category: Architecture, Engineering

Tags: Carbon Neutral, sustainable design, Timber, Building and Construction, Prototype

items.content_html: Maine grows 99% of the wild blueberries in the United States. 2025 was one of the worst harvest seasons this decade.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Wegovy linked to rare “eye stroke” that can cause sudden blindness
items.content_html: A new analysis is raising concerns about Wegovy, the blockbuster weight-loss drug, after researchers found it may carry the highest risk of a rare “eye stroke” that can cause sudden vision loss. The study, based on millions of FDA side-effect reports, found the risk signal was nearly five times stronger for Wegovy than for Ozempic, despite both containing semaglutide.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Scientists warn popular vitamin D supplement may have a “previously unknown” downside
items.content_html: A surprising study suggests vitamin D2 supplements may reduce the body’s levels of vitamin D3 — the more effective form of vitamin D. Researchers found D3 not only boosts vitamin D status more efficiently, but may also play a unique role in helping the immune system fight off viruses and bacteria. The discovery is prompting scientists to rethink whether D3 should become the preferred choice for supplementation.
